rationale: The transition structure ensures the robot advances when the path is clear, avoids collisions by turning away from obstacles, and systematically covers the area by switching to side coverage when blocked. Helper states like 'turning_left' and 'turning_right' guide systematic turns for coverage and prevent oscillations.
coverage_strategy: Favor moving forward when path is clear to maximize coverage efficiency.
When encountering an obstacle, rotate in the direction away from the obstacle (left if obstacle right, right if obstacle left, randomize if front).
Use helper states 'turning_left' and 'turning_right' to continue rotating until a clear path is detected, avoiding oscillation.
Disallow moving forward or random walk in any obstacle state to ensure safety.
Encourage systematic wall-following behaviors after obstacle encounters to avoid revisiting the same area.
Transitions always ensure a safe reaction to new obstacles, and helper states help prevent local cycling.
